turns.action: send 'What did I note about how Satya Nadella approached acquisitions at Microsoft?'
turns.response_text:
I'll be straightforward: **the vault doesn't have any episodes covering Satya Nadella.**

I ran multiple searches across every tool — `search_vault`, `search_vault_many` with three different angles, `search_transcript`, and `list_episode_ids` — and every result came back about Bill Gates, Steve Jobs, or other founders. Nadella doesn't appear in any episode title, transcript, or expanded note. The Founders podcast has covered the making of Microsoft extensively through Bill Gates (ep-0140, ep-0174) and Paul Allen (ep-0044), but hasn't yet done an episode on Nadella's tenure or his approach to acquisitions like LinkedIn, GitHub, or Mojang.

If you're interested in the closest adjacent material — how the Microsoft culture and competitive instincts were forged under Gates, which is the backdrop Nadella inherited — ep-0140 and ep-0174 cover that deeply. But there's nothing here yet on Nadella's own acquisition philosophy.
